verdict.answer_first: B leads with the usable outcome: "Rename a branch both places in four steps"; A opens with setup: "here's the standard sequence".
verdict.decisiveness: B gives one pasteable path: "git branch -m", "git push -u", "git push origin --delete"; A adds alternatives: "Equivalently" and a final one-liner offer.
verdict.fabrication: B has an internal mismatch: "four steps" but shows 3 numbered commands; no hard fabricated numbers in A.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it sets upstream in the same push: "git push -u origin new-name" before deleting old remote.
